title: STUNIR IR v1 dCBOR Canonicalization Spec (SPARK SSoT)

float_policy.rationale: Avoid cross-encoder divergence; IR is structural and should not contain floats.

normal_form._comment: AST-level normalization rules for mathematical confluence. Models MUST NOT invent their own formats.
normal_form.principle: Two semantically equivalent programs MUST produce identical canonical IR bytes.

field_ordering.rule: All object keys MUST be sorted lexicographically (UTF-8 byte order).
field_ordering.rationale: Ensures deterministic serialization independent of source order.

root_level.rule: Root arrays follow declaration order: types before functions.
root_level.rationale: Dependency resolution requires types before functions.

types_array.rule: Types sorted alphabetically by name field.

functions_array.rule: Functions sorted alphabetically by name field.

fields_array.rule: Struct fields sorted alphabetically by name field.
fields_array.rationale: Memory layout is target-dependent; canonical form uses logical ordering.

args_array.rule: Function arguments preserve source order (semantic significance).
args_array.rationale: Argument order affects calling convention; cannot be reordered.

alpha_renaming.scope: Bound variables within function bodies (not top-level names).
alpha_renaming.rule: Bound variables MUST use canonical names: _t0, _t1, _t2, ...
algorithm: 1. Traverse AST in depth-first, left-to-right order.
2. For each binding site (let, lambda, for-loop var), assign next _tN.
3. Replace all references to the original name with the canonical name.
4. Scopes are independent: same _tN may appear in sibling scopes.

example.input: let x = 1; let y = x + 2; return y

preserved_names.rule: Top-level names (module, function names, type names, field names) are NOT alpha-renamed.
preserved_names.rationale: These names are part of the public API and must be preserved for linking.

integers.rule: Encode as shortest CBOR integer representation.

strings.rule: MUST be NFC-normalized UTF-8 with no surrogates.

booleans.rule: Use CBOR simple values true (0xF5) and false (0xF4).

deduplication.rule: Duplicate type definitions with identical structure MUST be merged.

example.note: References to Vec2 rewritten to Point; first definition wins.

dead_code_elimination.rule: Unreachable functions MAY be removed (optional optimization).
dead_code_elimination.detection: Functions not referenced by exported functions or entry points.

constant_folding.rule: Constant expressions MUST be evaluated at IR construction time.

semantic_normalization._comment: Transformations that preserve semantics but change structure.

commutative_canonicalization.rule: Commutative operations MUST use canonical operand ordering.
commutative_canonicalization.ordering: Sort operands by their serialized CBOR bytes (lexicographic).

associative_flattening.rule: Associative chains MUST be flattened to left-associative form.

example.note: Left-associative nesting for deterministic tree shape.

alias_resolution.rule: Type aliases MUST be resolved to their underlying type.

platform_independence.rule: Platform-dependent types MUST normalize to explicit sizes.

control_flow_normalization.rule: Control flow structures normalize to canonical forms.

for_loop.normalized_form: while_loop with explicit init, condition, increment

example.output: { let i = 0; while (i < n) { body; i = i + 1; } }

switch_statement.normalized_form: chain of if-else with explicit fallthrough handling
switch_statement.note: Case values sorted by serialized CBOR bytes.

confluence_guarantee.theorem: The normal form rules are confluent: any sequence of valid transformations produces the same final IR.
proof_sketch: 1. All ordering rules are total orders (lexicographic).
4. No rule depends on external state or random choices.
confluence_guarantee.implication: Two implementations of these rules MUST produce identical canonical IR bytes for semantically equivalent programs.

versioning.backwards_compat_policy: Newer readers may support older formats; older readers may reject newer.
versioning.evolution: Bump id/version only when canonical encoding rules or IR model change impacts bytes.
